behavior_in_task_description.reason: All literal strings asserted in tests (remix#preview&path:packages/remix, remix#nightly, allowedOverwrites, ## Preview builds, preview branch name, --branch flag removal) are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes required end-state (no --branch flag, required positional arg, no allowedOverwrites) but doesn't dictate specific code changes or line edits.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_preview_workflow_builds_on_push asserts literal 'setup-installable-branch preview' substring and regex 'git push.*origin preview' — an alternative fix using a variable like BRANCH=preview then 'setup-installable-branch $BRANCH' or 'git push origin HEAD:preview' would fail despite being correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute node subprocesses that actually invoke util.parseArgs with the extracted config to verify flag rejection and branch resolution behavior, not just string matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require real behavioral changes (parseArgs config rejecting --branch, workflow triggering on push to main, file deletion); stubs wouldn't satisfy them.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All instruction requirements (workflow deletion, push trigger, flag removal, positional required, allowedOverwrites removal, JSDoc update, README/CONTRIBUTING updates) have corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with comment section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass) and have descriptive names and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies workflows/scripts/docs, not structured data outputs.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and strings are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to modify (appropriate scope) but doesn't dictate exact line edits.
